summary: Cross-chain mapping for the Harmony Horizon Bridge benchmark. Ethereum holds the custodial ETH/USDC/USDT/WBTC/DAI balance; Harmony chain (chain_id 1666600000) holds the wrapped representations. The attack drained the Ethereum-side custody — Harmony-side balances were unaffected, but the wrapped tokens on Harmony were left unbacked.

contracts.note: Harmony-side bridge contract. Receives wrapped representation mint instructions from the off-chain relayer when Ethereum-side `lock` events are observed. The 2022-06-23 attack drained the Ethereum custody only — Harmony-side balances were untouched, but the existing 1Inch / 1USDC etc. wrapped tokens on Harmony were left unbacked.

entity_map.role: Four ECDSA hot-wallet operators. Threshold 2-of-4 for any cross-chain action.
Validator-set governance + signature verification + withdrawal dispatch on Ethereum.
Custody pool — holds the locked ERC20 balances; releases only on manager call.
Harmony-side mint/burn router for wrapped representations.
Lazarus Group (DPRK) attacker EOA cluster; received the 11 batched withdrawals across multiple recipient addresses.

asset_map.notes: Native ETH leg, custodied as WETH on the bucket. ~$14M at attack-time prices.
Bundled in batched receipts alongside USDC/USDT/WBTC.

harmony.function_signature: withdraw / mint counterpart on Harmony chain
harmony.note: Harmony-side counterpart is informational; this benchmark fuzzes the Ethereum-side custody only.

message_map.message_id_field: digest = keccak256(abi.encode(target, value, data, nonce, address(this)))
message_map.proof_field: bytes[] sigs (>= 2 ECDSA signatures, ascending recovered-address order)

invariants_for_fuzzer.description: Every Unlocked event must correspond to a prior `execute` call carrying >= 2 valid distinct signatures from the configured operator set.
Only addresses in the constructor's signer list may contribute to the threshold quorum. (On-chain enforced; off-chain assumption 'fewer than 2 keys compromised' is what the incident broke.)
EthBucket.release reverts unless msg.sender == manager. The bucket trusts exactly one caller; the multi-sig validation lives upstream in the manager.
totalLocked[token] >= cumulative released[token]; bucket cannot release more than it has custodied for any single token, even across 11 batched receipts.

(Architectural / off-chain) Operator hot wallets must not be reachable from a single host or single phishing campaign. Production Harmony failed this — at least 2 of 4 keys lived close enough to a single attacker foothold to be compromised together.
